metric: mean test BCA over (subject, K>0) cells on source-pretrained MIRepNet; higher is better

bars_to_beat.note: from results/bciciv2a/mirepnet/modal_summary.json (teammate's runs). Will be re-measured locally for a same-pipeline comparison.

bars_to_beat_local_proxy14.note: SAME source-FT MIRepNet backbone as convex (fair). subj 1-4, K=[.5,1,2].

bars_to_beat_local_proxy14_250hz.note: SAME 250Hz source-FT backbone. subj1-4, K=[.5,1,2]. CONVEX MUST EXCEED THESE.

official_full_9subj_250hz.note: Definitive comparison, same source-FT MIRepNet backbone. LoRA leads overall; convex's proxy low-K 'win' was a subject-1 artifact.
official_full_9subj_250hz.verdict: LoRA > convex by 0.011 overall; convex wins only K=5; LoRA wins high-K decisively

status: WIN — LoRA+convex beats sft_lora on BOTH MIRepNet (proxy 0.611 vs 0.596) and NeuroGPT (full-dim 0.676 vs 0.656). Convex-head advantage GENERALIZES across foundation backbones (given full features; PCA bottleneck must be avoided). Convex-in-pretraining levers (transfer-anchor, coupling) negative; ensemble marginal. Backbone-agnostic recipe: source-FT FM -> (LoRA) -> full-dim convex ReLU head.

generality_experiments_frozen_proxy_9sub.note: cross-subject-generality objectives on FROZEN MIRepNet (K=[1,10,30]). None beats frozen-convex 0.582 or LoRA+convex 0.611.
generality_experiments_frozen_proxy_9sub.verdict: negative — pretrained backbone already cross-subject-general; per-target LoRA adaptation is the lever, not fixed-rule generality objectives

convex_in_pretraining_arc_iters14_18.note: Relaxed-harness arc (team-paper reference 2605.23235): two-stage convex transfer + convex-head ensemble, vs iter-8 LoRA+convex (full 0.595 / proxy 0.611).

convex_transfer_anchor.verdict: NEGATIVE — none beats its bar (frozen-convex 0.582 / LoRA+convex 0.611)
convex_transfer_anchor.finding: offline (source data available), POOLING raw source features dominates anchoring to a source-head summary; the anchor's value is the online/streaming regime, not this benchmark

convex_head_ensemble.vs_iter8_full: +0.0033 (within run-to-run noise); gains at K=5/30, small losses K=0.5/10/15; 3x compute
convex_head_ensemble.verdict: marginal — highest recorded full number but not a decisive win

convex_in_pretraining_arc_iters14_18.winner_unchanged: iter-8 LoRA+convex (full 0.595). convex_calib default stays n_ensemble=1, transfer_mode=none.

coupled_cronos_am_iters19_20.note: User hypothesis: couple LoRA<->convex head (vs iter-8's decoupled LoRA-then-convex). Alternate global convex solve <-> LoRA update through the fixed convex head (real CRONOS-AM w/ backbone via relaxed harness).
coupled_cronos_am_iters19_20.vs_iter8_proxy: -0.022 (worse at every K); identical across re-solve frequency -> moving-target ruled out
coupled_cronos_am_iters19_20.verdict: REFUTED — coupling underperforms the decoupled iter-8. Decoupling is load-bearing: LoRA wants a smooth co-adapting (linear) head as its SGD target; the convex head's value is global-optimal POST-HOC classification, not as a static training target (ill-conditioned: dead ReLUs / negative-W2 neurons).

implicit_diff_iters21_22.note: Implicit/unrolled differentiation through eq-2's KKT (dV*/dZ flows), since diffcp/cvxpylayers unavailable. Validated: forward argmax==jax, gradcheck rel-err 3e-7.
implicit_diff_iters21_22.verdict: REFUTED too. Full coupling family (fixed-head 0.5885/0.5886, implicit 0.5836/0.5870) clusters ~0.02-0.03 below iter-8 0.611; structural, not tuning. Convex head = great post-hoc classifier, poor representation-shaping objective.

neurogpt_cross_backbone_proxy_9subj.note: Baselines + best-3 on NeuroGPT (HF wenhuic/Neuro-GPT, 1024-d). Proxy 9 subj K=[1,10,30], shared source-FT. CORRECTED with full-dim convex.
neurogpt_cross_backbone_proxy_9subj.verdict: CONVEX WINS on NeuroGPT at FULL 1024-d: LoRA+convex 0.676 > sft_lora 0.656 (+0.020), frozen-convex 0.672 also > baseline. The earlier 'convex loses' was a PCA->256 artifact (~0.05 handicap). LoRA+convex beats sft_lora on BOTH MIRepNet (0.611 vs 0.596) AND NeuroGPT (0.676 vs 0.656) -> the convex-head advantage GENERALIZES (given full features). On NeuroGPT the convex head carries most credit (frozen-convex ~= LoRA+convex); on MIRepNet LoRA does.
neurogpt_cross_backbone_proxy_9subj.lesson: Never PCA-bottleneck the convex head on high-dim FMs; the 1024-d ADMM scales fine (~15s compile, 0.2s warm).

improved_2step_anchoring_kadaptive.note: User direction: improve 2-step anchoring; the a factor is very sensitive. FIX: data-relative prior anchor_a_mode=kadaptive, a_eff=a_base*n_ref/n_cal (auto-recedes as calibration grows). P:=a_base*n_ref is the effective knob.
improved_2step_anchoring_kadaptive.verdict: WIN on NeuroGPT: kadaptive anchor 0.6777 BEATS union (0.6715/0.6759) and baseline (0.6557); fixed-a anchor was 0.654. LOSES on MIRepNet: best 0.5746 < union 0.582. Backbone-dependent — anchoring wins when source is less task-aligned (NeuroGPT/TUH); pooling wins when source spans the task subspace (MIRepNet/MI). The data-relative prior universally fixes the a-sensitivity (smooth in P, no low-K crater).
